question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary's liveness check only reads fi.ModTime() from the inode metadata, which can be manipulated via utimes() syscall if the attacker has write access to /tmp, to backdate the heartbeat file and cause the liveness probe to fail, triggering a container restart?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not check whether the pinger's ticker (time.NewTicker(cfg.interval)) is still running, only whether /tmp/alive is fresh, to keep the liveness probe passing while the ticker has been stopped (e.g., due to a panic), causing a silent halt of transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's connection to the mirror node REST API, only the /tmp/alive heartbeat, to keep the probe passing while buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() has failed and the pinger is operating with a stale or empty network map, causing transaction routing failures?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary's readiness check (os.Stat('/tmp/ready')) does not verify that the pinger has successfully submitted at least one transaction, to mark the pod as ready before the pinger has established a working connection to the Hiero network, causing premature traffic routing to a non-functional pinger?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not implement any circuit breaker logic, meaning repeated liveness probe failures (each calling os.Exit(1)) will cause Kubernetes to restart the container indefinitely in a CrashLoopBackOff, preventing the pinger from ever reaching a stable state and confirming transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not check for disk space availability before relying on /tmp/alive being writable, to fill /tmp with large files, preventing the heartbeat goroutine from updating /tmp/alive, causing the liveness probe to detect a stale heartbeat and restart the container?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not validate the format or content of /tmp/alive (only its mtime), to replace the file with a zero-byte file or a file with invalid content while keeping its mtime fresh, masking a corrupted pinger state and hiding transaction confirmation failures?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary's liveness probe does not check the pinger's goroutine count or memory usage, to keep the probe passing while the pinger is in an OOM state or goroutine leak, causing degraded transaction submission performance that eventually leads to a total confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not implement any mutual exclusion with the pinger's heartbeat goroutine when reading /tmp/alive, to observe a partial write state where os.Stat returns stale metadata, causing a spurious liveness probe failure and an unnecessary container restart?
